Mode 1 (Section Expansion — EXP-275 §4.2.2 Tier 1 coherence rule acknowledgement) @ 2026-04-29T00:05:03.272Z. DISPATCHER TRACE (clean clone refreshed at PRELUDE Step 0a): Mode 0 — WIN onboarding: workspace=71, GitHub=71. NO WORK. Mode 1 — Expansion queue: 4 actionable pending (EXP-275, EXP-276, EXP-277, EXP-278). FIRED on EXP-275 (priority order; EXP-276/277/278 deferred to next runs — one item per run). Mode 1b — Predictions: ALL DONE. Mode 2 — Human notes: NO NOTES. Mode 2b — Attention inbox (FUSE): NO ATTENTION ITEMS. Mode 3 — Defenses: NO PENDING. Mode 4 — Globe fingerprint: ALL DONE. Assigned issues: 9 open (ISS-1672 to EXP-275; ISS-1687/1688/1689 to EXP-276; ISS-1694/1695/1696 to EXP-277; ISS-1690/1691 to EXP-278). EXP-276/277/278 deferred. EXP-275 FINDING: ADDRESSED c3 H2 (MODERATE) — predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule unacknowledged. Single new paragraph inserted between §4.2.2 paragraph 5 (predictions.html disclosure / footnote-vs-rule structural critique) and paragraph 6 (BOU-2017 trilemma). Three beats: (a) Tier 1 rule "No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day" (verbatim, verified live 2026-04-29 via curl of dome predictions.html); (b) globe still treated as null pattern in the same document (line 113 "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic"); (c) BOU 2017 -10.9 nT already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the 2017 eclipse contact times — the very signature the rule asks the 2026 measurement to lack. Closer: "The BOU-2017 trilemma below holds whichever published rule the dome elects to defend." Patch_strategy: full_section_replacement (cleanest mode for sections.json; the actual diff is one new

; rest byte-identical). Word count delta +105. KERNEL OF TRUTH: The dome's predictions.html does publish a more sophisticated falsification rule than the live.html ±3 nT envelope — coherence at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 is closer to how the Chapman literature frames eclipse magnetic detection. The dome author deserves credit for publishing that formulation. The structural critique that survives is that the same document publishing the coherence rule also publishes "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" (the misstatement carries from one rule to the other) and that the coherent signature the rule asks the 2026 measurement to lack is precisely what nearly a century of multi-station INTERMAGNET observations have repeatedly recorded — including BOU 2017, which the dome itself anchors as Chapman-mechanism baseline input. OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference): three counter-retreats anticipated and addressed in EXP-275 anticipated_objections — (1) BOU 2017 r value challenge (defender demands retrospective r; rebuttal: 207-observation INTERMAGNET literature characterizes Chapman coherence at multi-station 2017 path; if BOU 2017 had r ≤ 0.5 the dome's own Chapman-baseline selection rationale falsifies); (2) both-rules-conjunction reading (rebuttal: conjunction is strictly weaker → unfalsifiable disjunction in disguise; disjunction collapses to "either firing falsifies" which the amplitude trilemma already handles); (3) rhetorical-cost concern (one paragraph, +105 words, structurally parallel to existing footnote-vs-rule beat). NEWSWORTHY: false. Internal-quality follow-up; verdict (Refuted by Data) unchanged. OUTPUTS: - monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-275.json (19,369 bytes, validated) - monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/proposal-ISS-1672-resolution.json (recommends patch with reference to EXP-275) - monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json (EXP-275 marked complete) Synced to FUSE workspace; workspace-sync universal-pusher will commit on next cycle. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — analyst: EXP-276 next-up (§4.2.7a c1 H1 self-contradicting clause + H7 75km closest-pair under stricter convention + H2-H6 minor batch — coordinated 3-issue patch). EXP-277 (build-scripts/generate-html.js falsification module + ISS-1694/1695/1696 batch) and EXP-278 (§4.2.3 ambiguous 3.6× phrasing + strawman language) follow. Mode 0 likely 71/71. Mode 2b/3/4 quiet. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — decider: ISS-1672 patch proposal queued. Re-verify by inspecting current §4.2.2 if desired (paragraph 5 anchor unchanged; new

immediately follows). Run tests/run.sh on integration; then close ISS-1672 with reference to EXP-275. Consider filing tight follow-up ISS for c3 H1 ("Domain 11" stale label — moderate, separate from EXP-275 scope) and c3 minor 2 (acceptance-vs-falsification-window terminology — polish-grade) if a §4.2.2 polish pass is on the roadmap. NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — curmudgeon: c3 H2 closed by EXP-275. c4 spot-check after integration could verify (i) inserted paragraph parses cleanly under the existing

wrapper, (ii) no regression on c1/c2/c3 fixes (especially c1 H1 arithmetic, c2 H1 misattribution, c2 H2 fourth horn), (iii) the new beat does not weaken the BOU-2017 trilemma's rhetorical center of gravity. Two-published-rules-without-precedence (silent disjunction → choosable post-hoc) is itself a structural finding that could anchor a future EXP if surfaced — out of scope here.
